items.final_wording: Supports documenting the person’s understanding, perceived need, concerns, adverse-effect concerns, daily-routine fit, and questions during medicine review.

source.title: Medicines adherence: involving patients in decisions about prescribed medicines and supporting adherence
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: People prescribed medicines and healthcare professionals who prescribe, dispense, or review medicines.
source.setting: Shared medicine decisions, adherence assessment, support, and periodic medicine review.
source.exclusions: Exact for non-judgemental adherence history, missed-dose or medicine-taking behaviour documentation, beliefs, concerns, practical barriers, preferred support, and periodic review. It does not recommend a medicine, dose, change, or adherence intervention automatically.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.19–1.1.23 — knowledge, beliefs, and concerns about medicines

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting the person’s understanding, perceived need, concerns, adverse-effect concerns, daily-routine fit, and questions during medicine review.

items.rationale: Relevant to documenting the person’s knowledge, beliefs and concerns without inferring adherence.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting missed doses, stopping or restarting, intentional or practical barriers, and support preferences without generating an intervention.

source.title: Medicines adherence: involving patients in decisions about prescribed medicines and supporting adherence
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation

source.setting: Shared medicine decisions, adherence assessment, support, and periodic medicine review.
source.exclusions: Exact for non-judgemental adherence history, missed-dose or medicine-taking behaviour documentation, beliefs, concerns, practical barriers, preferred support, and periodic review. It does not recommend a medicine, dose, change, or adherence intervention automatically.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.8 — non-judgemental adherence assessment and tailored support

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting missed doses, stopping or restarting, intentional or practical barriers, and support preferences without generating an intervention.

items.rationale: Relevant to a clinician-recorded non-judgemental adherence discussion and identified barriers without generating support actions.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting periodic review of knowledge, concerns, need, and clinician prescribing decisions.
